topic: Why Your Stomach Doesn't Digest Itself

script.hook: Your stomach acid is strong enough to dissolve metal... so why doesn't it eat through your own body?
script.body: Every day, your stomach produces about 2 liters of hydrochloric acid — with a pH as low as 1.5. That's strong enough to dissolve a razor blade. But your stomach has a secret weapon: a thick layer of mucus, renewed every two weeks, that acts as an armor between the acid and your stomach wall. Specialized cells called mucous neck cells constantly pump out this protective gel. If this barrier ever fails — from stress, bacteria like H. pylori, or too much ibuprofen — that's when you get an ulcer. Your body is literally fighting a chemical war against itself every single day... and winning.
script.cta: Follow for more body secrets you never learned in school.
